branding.title: Branding
branding.subtitle: Make the hosted sign-in, sign-up, and account screens look like your own project.
branding.heroBody: Tell us how you want your auth experience to look and we'll build a branded theme for your apps. It's a premium feature — reach out and we'll take it from there.
branding.feature.css: Custom CSS for a pixel-perfect match
branding.contactHint: Opens an email to {{email}} with your project details prefilled.
branding.mailBody:
Hi ManyRows team,

We'd like to brand our sign-in experience for "{{project}}". Here's what we have in mind:

- Brand colors:
- Logo (attach or link):
- Fonts:
- Anything else:

Thanks!

profile.enterCodeToConfirm: Enter it below to confirm the change.

profile.totp.label: Two-factor authentication
profile.totp.scanDescription: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.).
profile.totp.confirmDescription: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to verify setup.
profile.totp.backupCodesWarning: Save these backup codes in a safe place. Each code can only be used once. If you lose access to your authenticator app, you can use a backup code to sign in.
profile.totp.disableDescription: Enter your password to disable two-factor authentication. Your account will only be protected by your password.
profile.totp.regenDescription: This will invalidate your existing backup codes and generate new ones. Enter your password to continue.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: API keys allow external systems to access your workspace. Keep them secret since they cannot be recovered once created.
apiKeys.limitReached: You can have a maximum of {{max}} API keys per workspace. Delete an existing key to create a new one.
apiKeys.createDescription: Choose a name to help you recognize this key later. You'll be shown the full key once after creation.
apiKeys.limitReachedCreate: API key limit reached ({{max}}). Delete an existing key to create a new one.
apiKeys.createdDescription: This is the only time you'll be shown the full API key. Copy it now and store it somewhere safe.
apiKeys.deleteDescription: This will permanently delete "{{name}}". This action cannot be undone.
apiKeys.editDescription: Change the name of this API key. The key itself will not be regenerated.
apiKeys.scopeHint: Read-only keys can only make GET requests — ideal for monitoring, analytics, or exports.
apiKeys.rotateDescription: Generate a new secret for "{{name}}" and invalidate the current one immediately. Any integration using the old key must be updated with the new one.
cors.title: CORS Origins
cors.description: This is for calling the ManyRows API directly from a browser (CORS). When a browser makes requests to the API, it will send Authorization: Bearer <JWT> on requests after you sign in.
cors.hint: Add the origins (scheme + host + optional port) you want to allow, like https://example.com or http://localhost:3000. No path/query.
cors.limitReached: You can have a maximum of {{max}} CORS origins per app. Delete an existing origin to add a new one.
cors.addDescription: Add an origin that's allowed to call the API from a browser. In JWT mode, the browser will send Authorization: Bearer <JWT> (and will often do a preflight OPTIONS request first). Enter a valid origin (scheme + host, optional port). No path/query.
cors.deleteDescription: This will remove "{{origin}}" from the allowlist.
cors.editDescription: Update the origin URL. Enter a valid origin (scheme + host, optional port). No path/query.
ipAllowlist.title: IP Allowlist
ipAllowlist.description: Restrict API access to specific IP addresses or CIDR ranges. When enabled, only requests from listed IPs will be allowed. If no IPs are configured, all IPs are allowed by default.
ipAllowlist.hint: Enter individual IPs (e.g., 203.0.113.50) or CIDR ranges (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8, 192.168.1.0/24).
ipAllowlist.limitReached: You can have a maximum of {{max}} IP allowlist entries per app. Delete an existing entry to add a new one.
ipAllowlist.noEntries: No IP restrictions configured. All IPs are currently allowed.
ipAllowlist.addDescription: Add an IP address or CIDR range that's allowed to access the API. Use CIDR notation for ranges (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24).
ipAllowlist.editDescription: Update the IP address or CIDR range and its description.
ipAllowlist.removeDescription: This will remove "{{ip}}" from the allowlist. Requests from this IP will be blocked if other IPs are still configured.
settings.nameTooLong: Name must be {{max}} characters or less.
settings.slugTooLong: Slug must be {{max}} characters or less.
encryption.title: Encryption Keys
encryption.intro: If you want to keep all your secrets with your Config Keys in one place, set up Encryption Keys. Secrets are encrypted in the browser before saving, so ManyRows never receives plaintext. Your server keeps the private key and decrypts secrets at runtime.
encryption.trustModel: Trust model: ManyRows stores your public key. Your team keeps the private key on your server to decrypt secrets at runtime.
encryption.setupDescription: Complete the steps in order. This ensures you never lose the private key.
encryption.step1.label: Generate key pair
encryption.step1.description: Generate an ECDH (P-256) key pair locally in your browser. ManyRows never receives the private key.
encryption.step2.label: Copy private key
encryption.step2.description: Copy the private key JSON and store it on your server (e.g. a secret manager). ManyRows cannot recover it later.
encryption.step2.required: Required step: copy the private key first. ManyRows never stores it.
encryption.step3.label: Save public key
encryption.step3.description: Save only the public key to ManyRows. This enables browser-side encryption for all secrets in this workspace.
encryption.step3.active: This workspace has an active encryption key configured.
encryption.complete.title: All steps completed
encryption.complete.description: Encryption keys are set up for this workspace. Store your private key securely on your server.
encryption.rotateWarning: Rotating the key means new secrets will be encrypted with the new public key. Ensure your server has the matching private key before rotating.
encryption.dialog.title: Private key (store this on your server)
encryption.dialog.warning: This private key is generated in your browser. ManyRows will never store it. Copy it now and save it in your server's secret manager. If you lose it, encrypted secrets cannot be recovered.
encryption.dialog.metadataNote: You do not need this metadata to decrypt secrets. It's only for confirming which key you saved.
encryption.dialog.privateKeyNote: Copy and store this JSON. This is the only data required to decrypt secrets.
encryption.snackbar.copied: Private key copied. Next: save the public key.
encryption.snackbar.clipboardBlocked: Clipboard blocked. Select the text and copy manually.
encryption.sdk.title: Decrypting secrets in your server
encryption.sdk.description: Use your private key to decrypt secrets at runtime. The Go module is `go get`-able; the Node, Python, and Java sources are on GitHub - copy the file into your project, or vendor the repo directly.
encryption.sdk.schemeDescription: Secrets are returned as JSON envelopes containing the ephemeral public key, IV, and ciphertext.
encryption.sdk.helpersNote: Each helper takes the raw envelope (object or JSON string) and your workspace private JWK, and returns the JSON-encoded plaintext.
encryption.sdk.securityReminder: Never expose your private key in client-side code or logs. Store it securely in your server's secret manager (e.g. AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or an environment variable).
validate.title: Validate email
validate.heading: One-time email verification
validate.description: We'll email a one-time verification code to confirm you own this address.
validate.codeHelper: Enter the 6-digit code from your email.
validate.sendPrompt: Click "Send code" to receive a verification code. You only need to do this once.
validate.error.invalidCode: Invalid code or expired. Please request a new code.
validate.error.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Please wait and try again.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Define permission slugs used by roles and access checks. Shared across every app (environment) in this project.
permissions.noPermissionsDesc: Permissions are the building blocks of access control. Define granular actions like
permissions.tip: Tip: Keep slugs stable - changing them can break permission checks in your UI and backend.
permissions.dialog.name: Name
permissions.dialog.slugHelperWithGroup: Use "resource:action" format. Will be grouped under "{{group}}".
permissions.dialog.editDisabled: Editing is disabled due to plan limits.
permissions.dialog.deleteWarning: This will delete "{{name}}". Any roles using it may need to be updated.
permissions.dialog.deleteWarningGeneric: This will delete this permission. Any roles using it may need to be updated.

roles.description: Define roles and assign permissions. Roles can be attached to users. Shared across every app (environment) in this project.
roles.dialog.roleNameHelper: Human-readable name shown in the UI
roles.dialog.slugHelper: Identifier used in code. Lowercase letters, numbers, underscores.
roles.tipFinal: Tip: Roles are used when assigning members to projects. Keep slugs stable.
roles.emptyState.title: No roles yet
roles.emptyState.description: Roles define what users can do in your project. Create roles like Admin, Editor, or Viewer, then assign permissions to each role.
roles.permDialog.title: Permissions - {{name}}
roles.permDialog.subtitle: Select what this role can do.
roles.permDialog.editDisabled: Editing is disabled due to plan limits.
roles.permDialog.info: Tick permissions to grant them to this role. Unticked permissions are not granted.
roles.permDialog.saveNote: Changes apply only after you click Save.
roles.permDialog.noPermissions: No permissions exist yet. Create permissions first before assigning them to roles.
roles.dupDialog.title: Duplicate role
roles.dupDialog.description: This will create a copy of {{name}} with all its permissions.
roles.dupDialog.descriptionFallback: This will create a copy of this role with all its permissions.
roles.dupDialog.renameHint: You can rename the role after it's created.
roles.delDialog.title: Delete role
roles.delDialog.editDisabled: Editing is disabled due to plan limits.
roles.delDialog.warning: You're about to delete {{name}}. This cannot be undone.
roles.delDialog.warningFallback: You're about to delete this role. This cannot be undone.
roles.delDialog.memberNote: Members assigned this role will lose its permissions. If your backend enforces referential integrity, you may need to remove member-role assignments first.
projectMembers.title: Users
projectMembers.reset2faBody: The user's two-factor authentication (authenticator app) will be removed. They'll sign in without a second factor until they set it up again — use this when a user has lost their authenticator.
projectMembers.unlockBody: Clears a failed-login lockout so the user can sign in again immediately.
projectMembers.setPasswordBody: Set a new password for this user; it must meet the app's password policy. The user can sign in with it immediately.
projectMembers.passwordTooWeak: Password doesn't meet the app's policy
projectMembers.magicLinkBody: A one-time sign-in link for this user (expires in 15 minutes). Copy it and send it to them securely.
projectMembers.magicLinkFailed: Failed to generate link (app must use magic-link sign-in)
projectMembers.createAppFirst: Create an app before adding users
projectMembers.createRoleFirst: Create a role before adding users
projectMembers.noAppDesc: Create an app before managing user access.
projectMembers.selectApp: Select an app above to view and manage user access.
projectMembers.noRolesDesc: Create a role before adding users to this app.
projectMembers.removeDialog.title: Remove user from app
projectMembers.dialog.noRolesWarning: No roles exist yet. Create a role first, then you can assign it.
projectMembers.dialog.selectRoleWarning: Select at least one role to save.
projectMembers.dialog.noRolesInfo: Saving with no roles selected will remove all roles for this user in {{app}}.
apps.dialog.deleteWarning: Are you sure you want to delete this app? This action cannot be undone.
apps.dialog.enabledDesc: Disabled apps cannot be accessed via the API or AppKit SDK.
apps.dialog.allowedDomainsHelper: Comma-separated list of allowed domains. Leave empty to allow all domains.
apps.dialog.allowRegDesc: Users can sign up without being invited
apps.require2faDesc: Require TOTP 2FA for password and Google sign-in
features.title: Feature Flags
features.scope.clientHelp: Can be delivered to frontend (NOT secure) and backend SDKs
features.info.title: Feature flags are for fast on/off control.
features.info.description: Use them to enable/disable features instantly per app, without redeploying. Flags are defined on the project (shared across all apps); each app toggles its own value per app. If you need values beyond a boolean (strings, numbers, JSON, lists), use Config Keys instead.
features.info.scope: Scope controls where the flag can be delivered: Server is backend-only; Client may be delivered to frontend (not secure).
features.noApps: No apps found for this project. Create apps first, then you can toggle flags per app.
features.selectApp: Select an app to view and edit values.
features.empty.title: No Feature Flags Yet
features.empty.description: Feature flags let you enable or disable features instantly without redeploying. Great for gradual rollouts, A/B testing, or emergency kill switches.
features.empty.tip1: Create a flag with a unique key (e.g., new_checkout_flow)
features.empty.tip2: Toggle it on/off per app (dev, staging, prod)
features.empty.tip3: Use the SDK to check the flag in your code
features.killSwitchTooltip: Kill switch: disable in ALL apps
features.prodConfirm.title: Are you sure?
features.prodConfirm.description: You're about to switch to {{app}}. This is production.
features.prodConfirm.warning: Changes you make here may affect live traffic. Double-check before saving.
features.dialog.keyHelper: Lowercase letters, numbers, underscore. Used by SDKs.
features.dialog.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{key}}?
features.dialog.killSwitchWarning: This will immediately disable the feature flag in ALL {{count}} apps, including production.
features.dialog.killSwitchHelp: Use this for emergencies when a feature is causing issues. You can re-enable the flag later.
features.dialog.enableAllWarning: This will immediately enable the feature flag in ALL {{count}} apps, including production.
features.dialog.enableAllHelp: This will make the feature available in all apps immediately. You can disable it per-app later.
features.snackbar.killSwitchActivated: Kill switch activated: "{{key}}" disabled in all apps
configKeys.title: Config Keys
configKeys.empty.title: No config keys yet
configKeys.empty.description: Config keys let you manage per-app settings like API URLs, feature limits, and secrets.
configKeys.selectEnvToEdit: Select an app above to edit values
configKeys.secretsRequireKey: Secrets require a workspace encryption key. Go to Security → Encryption Keys.
configKeys.exposure.publicDesc: Public values are safe to expose to client-side apps. Use for non-sensitive settings.
configKeys.exposure.privateDesc: Private values are server-only. They should never be exposed to client-side apps.
configKeys.exposure.secretDesc: Secret values are encrypted in the browser with your workspace public key and are write-only in the admin UI. You can set/rotate them, but they will never be shown again here. Your backend decrypts them at runtime using the matching private key - see the Encryption Key tab for the per-language SDK helpers.
configKeys.exposure.defaultDesc: Exposure controls where this value may be used and whether it can be read back.
configKeys.writeOnlyHelper: Write-only. Enter a new value to set/rotate.
configKeys.secretsRequireKeyShort: Secrets require a workspace encryption key.
configKeys.about.title: Config Keys are per-app settings (API URLs, limits, etc.). Keys are defined on the project (name, type, exposure - shared across all apps); values are set per app - that's what you're editing here.
configKeys.about.exposureControls: Exposure controls where values can be used:
configKeys.about.secretEncrypted: Encrypted in the browser, decrypted on your backend with our SDK (write-only here)
configKeys.about.secretsRequire: Secrets require a workspace encryption key. Open the Encryption Key tab to set one up.
configKeys.exposureInfo.public: Public values can be exposed to client-side apps. Use for non-sensitive config like UI labels, feature text, or public keys intended for the browser.
configKeys.exposureInfo.secret: Secret values are encrypted in the browser with your workspace public key and are write-only in the admin UI - you can set or rotate them, but they will never be shown again here. To use a secret, your backend pulls it from the delivery API and decrypts it at runtime with the matching private key. The Encryption Key tab links to the Go / Node / Python / Java decrypt helpers.
configKeys.exposureInfo.secretSetup: Setup required: Configure a workspace encryption key first (open the Encryption Key tab). Without a public key, ManyRows can't accept encrypted secret values.
configKeys.exposureInfo.private: Private values are server-only. They must never be exposed to client-side apps. Use for internal settings that aren't secrets but should stay backend-only.
configKeys.prodConfirm.title: Are you sure?
configKeys.prodConfirm.description: You're about to switch to {{app}}. This is production.
configKeys.prodConfirm.warning: Double-check what you're changing before saving. This can affect real users.
configKeys.dialog.keyHelper: Allowed: letters, numbers, underscore, dash, dot
configKeys.dialog.deleteDescription: This will delete the config key and all its app values.
configKeys.snackbar.invalidKey: Key must be non-empty and use only letters, numbers, _, -, .
configKeys.snackbar.encryptionKeyRequire: Workspace encryption key not configured (Security → Encryption Keys)
workspaceHome.accessRestrictedDesc: Only owners and admins can view or edit workspace settings. Ask an owner or admin to grant you access.
webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Manage webhook endpoints that receive event notifications.
webhooks.deleteConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this webhook? All delivery history will also be deleted.
webhooks.secretWarning: Copy this secret now. You won't be able to see it again.
webhooks.noFilteredDeliveries: No deliveries match the selected filter.
webhooks.maxWebhooks: This app already has a webhook configured.
sessions.pruneConfirmDescription: This will permanently delete all expired sessions for this workspace. Active sessions are not affected.
sessions.pruneNone: No expired sessions to clean up
wizard.getStarted.title: Get started
wizard.getStarted.description: Set up your first app in a few quick steps.
wizard.title: Quick Setup
wizard.error.projectFailed: Failed to create project. Please try again.
notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.
auth.turnstilePrompt: Let us know you are human
signingKeys.title: JWT signing keys
signingKeys.description: Rotate the ES256 keypair that signs end-user JWTs. New tokens sign with the new key; the previous key is kept in JWKS until you retire it, so tokens already in flight keep verifying. Wait at least the longest live refresh-token TTL (7 days by default, up to 30 days with remember-me, or whatever per-app override is set) before retiring the previous key.
signingKeys.rotateDialog.title: Rotate signing key?
signingKeys.rotateDialog.body1: A new ES256 keypair will be generated and start signing new JWTs immediately. The current key moves into the "previous" slot and stays in JWKS so in-flight tokens keep verifying. After you've waited long enough for those tokens to expire (≥ longest refresh-token TTL), retire the previous key to complete the rotation.
signingKeys.rotateDialog.body2: If you have multiple replicas behind a load balancer, only this replica reloads its in-memory keyset - redeploy after rotating so every replica picks up the new kid on the issuance path.
signingKeys.retireDialog.title: Retire previous key?
signingKeys.retireDialog.body: The previous key will be deleted from the database and dropped from JWKS. Any JWT still signed with it will fail verification with "unknown kid", forcing the user to re-authenticate. Only do this once you're confident every refresh-token issued before the rotation has expired.
passwordPolicy.intro: Applied when users set or reset a password. Existing passwords aren't re-checked - the policy only gates new ones.
passwordPolicy.zxcvbnInfo: We don't expose the deprecated "must contain uppercase / digit / symbol" knobs. Strength is scored with zxcvbn, which catches dictionary words, keyboard patterns, and personal-info reuse - the categories that actually fall to attacks.
passwordPolicy.minLengthHelp: NIST recommends a floor of 8. 12+ is stronger and still survivable for memorisation; 16+ assumes a password manager.
passwordPolicy.minScoreHelp: Score 2 rejects "password123", "qwerty", and trivial substitutions. Score 3+ starts blocking many human-memorable passwords - pair with a password-manager-only audience.
passwordPolicy.reuseHint: Blocks the 5 most recently used passwords when users set or reset a password.
cookieDomain.title: Cookie domain
cookieDomain.description: Per-app override for the session-cookie Domain attribute. When set, this takes precedence over the workspace value - useful if you have apps on different parent domains in the same install.
cookieDomain.workspaceValueNotSet: not set (browser scopes cookies to the exact host)
cookieDomain.workspaceValueSuffix: . Anything you enter below overrides this for this app only. Leave blank to inherit the workspace value.
cookieDomain.suggestionBody: Auth domain is set to {{authDomain}}. Without a Cookie domain, session cookies are scoped host-only to that subdomain - your API endpoints on {{suggested}} won't see them. Set Cookie domain to {{suggested}} so the cookies ride to both. Note: any sibling subdomain (e.g. {{sibling}}) will also receive the cookie.
cookieDomain.fieldInvalid: Must be a hostname (no spaces or slashes) and typically contain a dot.
bruteForce.intro: Detects and blocks repeated failed login attempts to prevent unauthorized access through credential guessing. Applies to this app's user sign-in only.
bruteForce.enableDesc: When off, failed-login lockout and login rate limiting are disabled for this app. Other abuse protections (password reset, magic link, email send limits) stay active.
sessionLifetime.intro: How long an end-user's session stays valid before they have to sign in again.
sessionLifetime.existingNote: Existing sessions stay alive until they reach the new absolute limit; nothing is invalidated retroactively.
sessionTransport.intro: How the session token is delivered to the user's browser. AppKit picks up this setting from the boot response, so the SDK doesn't need a separate prop on the customer side.
sessionTransport.localDesc: ManyRows issues a JWT to your frontend, which keeps it in localStorage and sends it as a Bearer token. Simplest to set up - no backend code, no DNS - but the token is visible to any JS running on your origin.
sessionTransport.cookieTitle: First-party cookie (same-host deploy OR custom-domain CNAME)
sessionTransport.cookieDesc: ManyRows sets an HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax session cookie on a host that's same-site as your app (e.g. auth.yourdomain.com alongside www.yourdomain.com). The token never touches JS. Works whether ManyRows is reached via a custom-domain CNAME or a same-host deploy. Configure the cookie domain below; setup details for the custom-domain shape are in the dialog.
sessionTransport.cookieLocalDevNote: Local development: keep Local (browser storage). Cookie mode is same-site only, so it can't authenticate a localhost app against a hosted ManyRows install (they're cross-site). Test cookie mode in a same-site staging deploy instead.
sessionTransport.dirtyWarning: You've picked a different transport than what's currently configured. The change isn't applied until you save - switching modes also clears the per-mode setup panel below so you can configure the new mode after committing.
sessionTransport.noSetupBody: Local mode is the AppKit default. There's nothing to configure here - embed AppKit on your site and sign-in just works. To switch later, pick First-party cookie above.
sessionTransport.customDomainBody: If ManyRows isn't already on a same-site host with your app, you can CNAME a subdomain (e.g. auth.yourdomain.com) to this install. The dialog covers DNS, TLS, and Cloudflare-specific gotchas.
cookieStrictness.title: Cookie strictness
cookieStrictness.description: SameSite attribute on the session cookies. Lax (the default) works with every auth flow. Strict adds a marginal CSRF hardening but breaks any flow that lands the user on this app via a top-level cross-site GET - magic links, OAuth redirects, link-based password resets.
cookieStrictness.laxDesc: Cookies ride along on top-level navigation from another site. Required for magic links, OAuth, and any "click a link in email → land logged in" flow.
cookieStrictness.strictDesc: Cookies are sent only on same-site requests. A user who clicks a link from another site lands logged-out and has to navigate within this app once before the cookie returns.
cookieStrictness.strictDisabledNote: Strict requires no link-based auth flows. Currently active: {{blockers}}. Disable those before switching to Strict.
customDomain.title: Custom domain setup
customDomain.step3: 3. Add the Custom Hostname in Cloudflare
customDomain.originRulesNote: Origin Rules (Host + SNI rewrite) are Enterprise-only. The Worker below does the same thing on Free/Paid plans for a few dollars a month.
projectMembers.clearPwBody: The user's password will be unset. They can no longer sign in with email + password until they reset via the forgot-password flow. OAuth and passkey sign-in are unaffected.
projectMembers.clearPwSessions: All active sessions for {{email}} will also be revoked.
projectMembers.importNote: Roles and field values will be applied. Unknown role/permission/field slugs are silently skipped; check the result for errors.
apps.dialog.googleSecretPlaceholder: Required - paste from Google Cloud Console
apps.dialog.microsoftSecretPlaceholder: From Entra → Certificates & secrets → New client secret → Value
apps.dialog.githubSecretPlaceholder: Generate a new secret in your OAuth App settings
apps.accountDeletion.title: Account deletion
apps.accountDeletion.desc: Controls whether end users can delete their own account from the AppKit profile dialog. Disable when you want to keep audit trails intact and route deletions through support.
apps.accountDeletion.switchLabel: Allow users to delete their own account
apps.accountDeletion.switchCaption: When off, the "Delete account" button is hidden and the server rejects deletion requests with 403.
apps.emailChange.title: Email change
apps.emailChange.desc: When on, AppKit's profile dialog shows a "Change Email Address" block; the user enters their current password + new address, receives a verification code at the new address, and the change applies on confirm. When off, the block is hidden and the server rejects email-change requests with 403.
apps.emailChange.warningBody: Email is usually the canonical identity tying your audit logs and support tickets to a user - letting end users rewrite it from the profile dialog breaks that link and creates support headaches. Only enable when you have a specific reason (e.g. internal tool where users routinely change addresses). Only applies when email/password sign-in is on - the change-email flow needs a current password to confirm, so it's hidden automatically in code or OAuth-only mode regardless of this toggle.
apps.emailChange.switchLabel: Allow users to update their email address
externalIdp.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}"? Users who signed in through it will need to re-link.
externalIdp.title: External identity providers
externalIdp.desc: Let users sign in through any OIDC or OAuth2 provider — Okta, Auth0, Keycloak, Entra, GitLab, Discord, and more. Each appears as its own button on the sign-in screen.
externalIdp.slugHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens. Used in the URL and as the button id; can't change later.
externalIdp.issuerUrlHelp: We fetch /.well-known/openid-configuration from here. Must be https.
externalIdp.scopesHelp: Space-separated. Defaults to 'openid email profile' if blank.
externalIdp.buttonIconHelp: Optional icon name (key, shield, lock, fingerprint, ...).
externalIdp.trustUnverifiedWarning: Only enable this for an IdP that verifies emails but doesn't send the email_verified claim. Enabling it for a provider where users can set arbitrary emails risks account takeover.
features.roleTargeting.title: Role Targeting
features.roleTargeting.description: Select roles that should see this flag as enabled. Leave empty for all users.
configKeys.validation.boolNotText: bool should not be parsed from text
sso.subtitle: Let a user sign in once and carry that session across the apps you choose.
sso.appsNotice: Users are handled by the apps within a project, not at the workspace level. Single sign-on across those apps is coming soon.
sso.empty.title: Single sign-on is on the way
sso.empty.description: You'll be able to pick a set of apps and let a user log in once for all of them — one session, shared identity, no re-prompt as they move between apps.
signingKeys.subtitle: Rotate the ES256 keypair that signs end-user JWTs. New tokens sign with the new key; the previous key is kept in JWKS until you retire it, so tokens already in flight keep verifying.
signingKeys.superOnly: Only super-admin accounts can rotate signing keys.
cookieDomain.subtitle: Sets the Domain attribute on session cookies for every app in this workspace. Use the parent-domain form (e.g. .acme.com) when ManyRows and your app share a registrable domain - the session cookie then survives a top-level redirect between subdomains. Leave blank to scope cookies to the exact host that issued them. Cookies are same-site only; cross-origin apps fall back to Bearer-token (local) transport.
cookieDomain.label: Cookie domain
cookieDomain.invalid: Must be a hostname (no spaces or slashes) and typically contain a dot.
cookieDomain.emptyHint: Empty = scope cookies to the exact host that set them.
settings.slugPropagates: The slug appears in URLs and in the AppKit boot response, so changes propagate to every running session.
smtp.relayDescription: Configure a workspace-level relay to send password resets, magic links, and verification codes from your own domain. When off, the install's system transport is used.
workspaceUsers.title: All users
workspaceUsers.subtitle: Every account across this workspace's pools. The same email can appear more than once when it exists in more than one pool. Click an app chip to jump to that app's user list.
workspaceUsers.empty.title: No users in this workspace yet.
workspaceUsers.empty.description: Users appear here once apps in this workspace start signing people in.
userFields.col.label: Label
userFields.field.label: Label
userFields.userEditable.allow: Allow users to update this field via the client SDK
authLogs.noResults: No auth events match these filters.
authLogs.col.subject: Subject
authLogs.drawer.subject: Subject
authLogs.drawer.showSessionEvents: Show all events for this session →
authLogs.drawer.showRequestEvents: Show all events for this request →
appInsights.empty.logins: No login data - events start collecting once users sign in
appInsights.title: Insights
apps.appleConfigDesc: Apple Developer credentials. Services ID is the OAuth client_id; the .p8 private key is used to mint short-lived ES256 client-secret JWTs at sign-in time.
apps.appleConfigIncomplete: Fill all Apple credential fields before enabling.
apps.appleConfigRequired: Fill all four credential fields below first.
apps.detail.accessTokenTtlDesc: Trades JWT-replay window against refresh-call frequency. Don't drop below ~5 minutes unless your SDK can handle refresh storms. Leave blank for the default (15 minutes).
apps.detail.setupAuthDesc: Add sign-in and user accounts — only if your app needs them.
apps.detail.appUrlDesc: Used in emails such as password reset and invite links
apps.detail.appUrlRequired: App URL is required and must start with http:// or https://
apps.detail.changePoolHint: Point this environment at a different pool of users. Existing sessions may be invalidated.
apps.detail.authDomainHelp: Custom domain that serves this app's hosted auth pages (optional).
apps.detail.userPoolHelp: The pool of users this environment authenticates against.
apps.detail.idleTimeoutDesc: Refresh is refused when the session hasn't been active for this many minutes; the session then dies once the current access token expires. Leave blank to disable idle enforcement.
apps.detail.maxSessionsDesc: Logging in beyond this cap prunes the user's oldest session for this app. Set 1 for single-device apps; raise it for productivity tools where users span phone + laptop + tablet. Leave blank for the default (5).
apps.detail.sessionTtlDesc: Absolute lifetime. Leave blank for the default (7 days / 10080 minutes).
apps.dialog.appleKeyIdHelp: 10-character Key ID for the Sign in with Apple key
apps.dialog.applePrivateKeyHelp: Paste the contents of the .p8 file from Apple Developer. Encrypted at rest.
apps.dialog.appleRedirectUriHelp: Add this to Apple Developer → Services ID → Sign in with Apple → Return URLs
apps.dialog.appleServicesIdHelp: From Apple Developer → Identifiers → Services IDs
apps.dialog.appleTeamIdHelp: 10-character Team ID from Apple Developer membership page
apps.dialog.githubClientIdHelp: From GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps → Client ID
apps.dialog.githubClientSecretHelp: Encrypted at rest. GitHub only shows the secret once at generation time - copy it from the OAuth App settings page right after creating it.
apps.dialog.googleClientIdHelp: From Google Cloud Console → Credentials → OAuth 2.0 Client ID
apps.dialog.googleClientSecretHelp: Required. ManyRows exchanges the OAuth code server-to-server with this secret.
apps.dialog.googleRedirectUriHelp: Add this to Google Cloud Console → Credentials → Authorized redirect URIs
apps.dialog.kakaoClientIdHelp: From Kakao Developers → My Application → App Keys → REST API key
apps.dialog.kakaoClientSecretHelp: Encrypted at rest. Generate and activate it under Kakao Login → Security → Client Secret on your Kakao app.
apps.dialog.kakaoRedirectUriHelp: Register this under Kakao Login → Redirect URI on your Kakao app.
apps.dialog.microsoftClientIdHelp: From Microsoft Entra → App registrations → Application (client) ID
apps.dialog.microsoftClientSecretHelp: Use the Value column from the secret you created in Entra (not the Secret ID). Encrypted at rest.
apps.dialog.microsoftRedirectUriHelp: Add this to Entra → App registrations → Authentication → Web → Redirect URIs
apps.dialog.microsoftTenantHelp: Common is the broadest reach. Organizations is the safer SaaS default - blocks personal Outlook accounts. Single tenant restricts sign-in to one specific Entra organization.
apps.dialog.microsoftTenantIdHelp: From Entra ID Overview → Directory (tenant) ID. Only users in this organization can sign in.
apps.dialog.microsoftTenantIdInvalid: Must be a UUID (Directory ID) from your Entra tenant.
apps.dialog.naverClientIdHelp: From Naver Developers → your application → Overview → Client ID
apps.dialog.naverClientSecretHelp: Encrypted at rest. Shown next to the Client ID in your Naver application overview.
apps.dialog.naverRedirectUriHelp: Register this under your Naver application → API Settings → Callback URL.
apps.dialog.naverTrustUnverifiedEmailHel: Naver doesn't report whether a user verified their email, so ManyRows refuses Naver sign-in until you turn this on. Only enable it if you accept signing users in (and linking existing accounts) by a Naver-supplied email.
apps.githubConfigDesc: OAuth App credentials from GitHub Developer Settings. Users sign in with GitHub and we use their primary verified email as the account identifier.
apps.githubConfigIncomplete: Configure both Client ID and Client Secret before enabling GitHub sign-in.
apps.githubConfigRequired: Configure Client ID + Client Secret below first.
apps.googleClientIdRequired: Enter a Client ID before enabling Google sign-in.
apps.googleClientSecretRequired: Enter a Client Secret before enabling Google sign-in.
apps.googleConfigDesc: Credentials from Google Cloud Console. Both Client ID and Client Secret are required - sign-in uses the OAuth Authorization Code Flow, which does a server-to-server token exchange.
apps.googleConfigRequired: Configure the Client ID and Client Secret below first.
apps.kakaoConfigDesc: Credentials from your Kakao Developers app. ManyRows verifies the Kakao OpenID Connect id_token and uses the account email as the identifier.
apps.kakaoConfigIncomplete: Configure both REST API key and Client Secret before enabling Kakao sign-in.
apps.kakaoConfigRequired: Configure REST API key + Client Secret below first.
apps.kakaoPrereqTitle: Set up on the Kakao Developers side first
apps.microsoftConfigDesc: Application credentials from Entra ID (Azure AD). The tenant scope decides which kinds of Microsoft accounts can sign in.
apps.microsoftConfigIncomplete: Configure Client ID and Client Secret before enabling Microsoft sign-in.
apps.microsoftConfigRequired: Configure Client ID + Client Secret below first.
apps.microsoftTenantInvalid: Tenant ID must be a UUID, or pick one of the preset scopes.
apps.microsoftXmsEdovTitle: Required: enable the xms_edov optional claim in Entra
apps.naverConfigDesc: Credentials from your Naver Developers application. Naver is OAuth2-only; ManyRows reads the account email from Naver's userinfo endpoint and uses it as the identifier.
apps.naverConfigIncomplete: Configure both Client ID and Client Secret before enabling Naver sign-in.
apps.naverConfigRequired: Configure Client ID + Client Secret below first.
apps.naverPrereqBody: 1) Register a Naver Login application at developers.naver.com and paste its Client ID + Client Secret below. 2) Add the email consent item and mark it Required — Naver only returns an email the user agreed to share, and ManyRows refuses a Naver sign-in with no email. 3) Register the Callback URL below under the application's API settings.
apps.naverPrereqTitle: Set up on the Naver Developers side first
apps.noneDesc: An app is a sign-in surface - usually one per environment (prod, staging, dev).
apps.oauth.intro: Add social sign-in providers. Click a provider to configure its credentials; each provider's own switch enables or disables it.
apps.oidc.clearConfirmDesc: Clearing the secret turns this app into a public client (PKCE-only). Existing integrations that send a client_secret at /token will fail until reconfigured.
apps.oidc.clientIdHelp: Stable per app. Send as client_id at /authorize and /token.
apps.oidc.clientSecretDesc: Confidential clients (backend code) authenticate at /token with a secret. Public clients (browser/mobile, PKCE-only) leave the secret empty.
apps.oidc.cookieModeRequired: OIDC requires Cookie transport mode. Switch this app's transport mode in the General tab before enabling.
apps.oidc.desc: Expose this app over standard OpenID Connect so any off-the-shelf OIDC client library (next-auth, passport-openidconnect, Spring Security, etc.) can authenticate users without the ManyRows SDK. Coexists with the SDK path — both work in parallel.
apps.oidc.discoveryUrlHelp: Most OIDC libraries take this single URL and self-configure.
apps.oidc.enableLabel: Enable OIDC provider for this app
apps.oidc.endpointsDesc: Paste these into your OIDC client library configuration.
apps.oidc.invalidUri: Redirect URI must be a valid http(s) URL
apps.oidc.issuerUrlHelp: The iss claim on issued id_tokens. Matches the discovery doc's issuer field.
apps.oidc.postLogoutDesc: Optional. Exact-match allowlist for post_logout_redirect_uri on /oidc/end-session.
apps.oidc.redirectUrisDesc: Exact-match allowlist. The RP's redirect_uri at /authorize must match one of these exactly.
apps.oidc.redirectUrisRequired: Add at least one Redirect URI before enabling OIDC.
apps.oidc.redirectUrisRequiredHint: Add at least one Redirect URI before saving.
apps.oidc.secretRevealedDesc: Copy this secret and store it somewhere safe. It will NEVER be shown again — only its hash is kept. If you lose it, generate a new one (which invalidates this one).
apps.oidc.title: OpenID Connect provider
apps.passkeyDesc: Set the Relying Party ID - the registrable domain users will register passkeys for. Must be a registrable suffix (eTLD+1 or a subdomain of one) of every CORS origin on this app. Leave blank to disable passkeys.
apps.passkeyDisabled: Passkeys are currently disabled for this app.
apps.passkeyHint: Examples: example.com (covers app.example.com + staging.example.com), or app.example.com to scope passkeys to that subdomain only. Use 'localhost' for local development.
apps.passkeyRPIDChangeWarning: Changing the RPID will invalidate every passkey already registered for this app - they're cryptographically bound to the old domain. Affected users will need to re-register before they can sign in with a passkey. The other auth methods are unaffected.
apps.primaryAuth.codeDesc: Passwordless. Users enter their email, receive a 6-digit code, and sign in. Uses the default mailer unless custom SMTP is configured.
apps.primaryAuth.magicLinkDesc: Passwordless. Users enter their email and receive a one-click sign-in link. Requires an App URL (the link points back to your app).
apps.primaryAuth.magicLinkNeedsAppUrl: Set an App URL on the app's main page to enable this option.
apps.primaryAuth.noneDesc: Hide the email field entirely. At least one OAuth provider must be enabled.
apps.primaryAuth.passwordDesc: Standard password sign-in. Users without a password set fall back to a one-time code over email.
apps.primaryAuthDesc: Pick how the email field behaves on the sign-in screen. Password, sign-in code, and magic link are mutually exclusive - only one form can be shown. Choose “No email form” if this app is OAuth-only.
apps.qr.appUrlRequired: Set App URL under General → App URL before enabling QR sign-in.
apps.qr.appUrlRequiredHint: QR sign-in requires App URL to be set (under General → App URL). The desktop success flow redirects there with tokens in the URL fragment; without it there's nowhere safe to land.
apps.qr.desc: Lets users sign in on a desktop by scanning a QR code with their phone. The phone (already signed in) approves; the desktop receives tokens. AppKit renders a 'Sign in with phone' button on the login screen when this is on.
apps.qr.enableLabel: Enable QR sign-in for this app
apps.qr.integrateDesc: Link to this from your app to bootstrap the QR flow. After the user scans + approves on their phone, tokens land in the URL fragment at the return_to target.
apps.qr.title: Cross-device sign-in (QR)
apps.qr.urlHelp: Append ?return_to=<your-callback> when linking. return_to host must match App URL.
apps.qr.urlPending: URL will appear once MANYROWS_BASE_URL is pinned (happens on first admin register).
apps.twoFactorDesc: Cross-cutting policy that applies to every sign-in method on this app.
auth.register.firstAdmin: First time here - whoever registers next becomes the super admin for this install.
auth.register.termsPrefix: By continuing, you agree to the
projectMembers.bulkDisableBody: Disabled users can't sign in. They keep all roles and data - re-enable any time.
projectMembers.bulkEnableBody: Re-enables sign-in for the selected users.
projectMembers.bulkPartialFailure: {{ok}} succeeded, {{fail}} failed. Review and retry below.
projectMembers.bulkRemoveBody: Removes the selected users from this app — their roles, permission overrides, and sessions for this app are cleared. Their account and pool data are kept.
projectMembers.dialog.sendInviteNoUrl: Set an App URL in app settings first
projectMembers.inviteEmailFailed: User added, but the invite email could not be sent.
projectMembers.inviteEmailFailedReason: User added, but the invite email failed: {{reason}}
projectSettings.deleteHint: Removes the project, its apps, and all associated data. This cannot be undone.
projectSettings.deleteWarning: This permanently deletes the project, its apps, users, and roles. This action cannot be undone.
setup.email.subtitle.done: A test email was delivered successfully.
setup.email.subtitle.todo: Send a test from Email Settings to verify your transport and sender address work.
setup.email.title: Email delivery verified
setup.firstApp.subtitle.todo: Run the Quick Start wizard from the Get Started panel below.
setup.firstApp.title: First app created
setup.superAdmin.subtitle: You're signed in as the super-admin.
setup.superAdmin.title: Super-admin account created
setup.title: Complete your setup
smtp.system.active.body: Outbound mail is being delivered automatically - system-level credentials are configured. The form below overrides per-workspace.
smtp.system.console.body: Sign-in and admin emails are being logged to stdout instead of delivered. Set MANYROWS_SMTP_* env vars or fill in the SMTP form below.
smtp.system.console.title: No outbound email transport is configured.
smtp.system.fromMissing: MANYROWS_FROM_EMAIL is not set; the server will refuse to send outbound mail until you configure a sender on your own domain.
userActivity.noLogins: No logins in the last 30 days
userActivity.title: Activity
userFields.descriptionPool: Define custom fields for users in this pool. Values are stored per user.
userFields.noFieldsDesc: Add custom fields like display name, role, or any per-user attributes your app needs.
userFields.title: User Fields
userPools.col.name: Name
userPools.deleteBlocked: Remove or repoint apps using this pool first
userPools.detail.noApps: No apps point at this pool. Safe to delete, or repoint a new app here to start using it.
userPools.detail.noUsers: No users in this pool yet.
userPools.detail.purgeOrphansConfirm: Permanently deletes every user in this pool that belongs to no app, with all of their data. Users who are members of any app are not affected. This cannot be undone.
userPools.detail.purgeOrphansTitle: Delete all users with no apps?
userPools.detail.subtitle: Identity boundary. Apps pointing here share users.
userPools.dialog.nameHelp: Shown only to admins. Apps point at the pool by ID.
userPools.noneDesc: Pools are usually auto-created on app create. Make one here when you want two apps to share users before either exists.
userPools.subtitle: Identity boundaries. Apps pointing at the same pool share users - the basis for SSO between related apps.
userPools.title: User pools
userTags.help: Free-form labels - type to add, click × to remove. Tags are lowercased and trimmed automatically.
userTags.title: Tags
webhooks.noWebhooksDesc: Receive HTTP callbacks when events happen in this app - sign-ins, sign-ups, sessions, and more.
wizard.appEnv.help: Add the other environments later - prod / staging / dev share roles, permissions, config keys, and feature flags under one project.
wizard.appEnv.label: Environment
wizard.appUrl.help: Where end-users access your app. Used for invite emails, password-reset links, and OAuth redirects.
wizard.appUrl.invalid: Must start with http:// or https://
wizard.appUrl.label: App URL
wizard.primaryAuth.help: How end-users sign in by default. You can change this and add OAuth providers (Google, Apple, etc.) later.
wizard.primaryAuth.label: Primary sign-in method
wizard.projectName.help: The project this belongs to. Each environment - prod, staging, dev - is a separate app under it.
wizard.projectName.label: Project name
wizard.userPool.helpExisting: Apps sharing a pool share users (SSO). Pick a pool another app already uses.
wizard.userPool.helpNew: Default. Users created in this app do not exist in any other app.
wizard.userPool.label: User pool
wizard.userPool.optionExisting: Share users with an existing pool
userFields.deleteConfirm: Delete field "{{key}}"? This cannot be undone.
apps.detail.rememberMeTtlDesc: Applied when the user opted into "Keep me signed in" at login. Leave blank for the default (30 days / 43200 minutes).
apps.dialog.githubRedirectUriHelp: Paste this into the OAuth App's "Authorization callback URL" field on GitHub.
apps.kakaoPrereqBody: 1) The Client ID below is your Kakao app's REST API key. 2) Turn on "Activate OpenID Connect" (Kakao Login → project settings). 3) Enable the Client Secret (Security) and paste it below. 4) Add the account_email consent item and set it Required — Kakao requires Business verification (사업자등록) to release email. Kakao only shares an email once it is verified, and ManyRows refuses a Kakao sign-in with no email.
apps.microsoftXmsEdovBody: When the tenant scope is Common, Organizations, or Consumers, ManyRows requires the xms_edov claim (Email Domain Owner Verified) to defend against the multi-tenant email-spoofing pattern. xms_edov isn't emitted by default and isn't visible in Entra's standard Token Configuration UI - your tenant admin must add it via Graph API or by editing the app manifest. Without it, every Microsoft sign-in to this app will fail. Pick "Single tenant" instead if you want to skip this requirement (the customer trusts only their own tenant).
userPools.detail.deleteUserConfirm: Permanently deletes "{{email}}" and all of their data (roles, permissions, identities, sessions, custom fields). This cannot be undone.
userPools.dialog.deleteConfirm: This permanently removes "{{name}}" and every user in it. Apps must be repointed or deleted first.
projectMembers.disableBody: This user will no longer be able to log in. All active sessions will be revoked.
projectMembers.enableBody: This user will be able to log in again.
